interop_matrix.notes: Confirms runtime bootstrap dialing against public Filecoin peers.

interop_matrix.focus: identify, ping, and advertised Filecoin protocol IDs
interop_matrix.notes: Records negotiated transport/security/muxer metadata where available.

interop_matrix.focus: identify and ping over an explicit Lotus multiaddr
interop_matrix.notes: Reproducible by operators, but not launched in CI by this module.

interop_matrix.focus: identify and ping over an explicit Forest multiaddr
interop_matrix.notes: Same probe path as Lotus, using the existing Filecoin examples.

interop_matrix.focus: advertised Hello and ChainExchange protocol IDs
interop_matrix.notes: Checks protocol advertisement, not full runtime protocol exchange.

interop_matrix.focus: advertised Hello and ChainExchange protocol IDs
interop_matrix.notes: Checks protocol advertisement, not full runtime protocol exchange.

interop_matrix.focus: read-only observer mode over Filecoin topics
interop_matrix.notes: Observer mode is intentionally safe/read-only and may log expected peer-specific failures.
Module 1 documents the gap instead of shipping a stub implementation.
Protocol IDs are present; request/response behavior remains follow-up work.

network_parity_audit.implication: Generic py-libp2p defaults do not imply QUIC or WebTransport parity for Filecoin nodes.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Document Filecoin-specific listen-address recommendations rather than changing the global host default in this module.

network_parity_audit.implication: TCP interop is straightforward, but broader Filecoin transport defaults are not mirrored by a plain py-libp2p host.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Keep the transport metadata visible in interop probe outputs and document the Filecoin-oriented transport settings.

network_parity_audit.implication: Noise-first operation interoperates, but exact ordering/config shape differs from Lotus configurability.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Expose negotiated security protocol in probe output and recommend Filecoin-specific security choices in docs.

network_parity_audit.implication: Yamux-first aligns well for TCP, but py-libp2p still keeps a generic Mplex fallback path.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Keep Yamux-first and capture the negotiated muxer during probes.

network_parity_audit.implication: Ping and identify diagnostics already provide a good interoperability surface for Filecoin nodes.

network_parity_audit.concern: Connection manager thresholds and grace period
network_parity_audit.implication: py-libp2p defaults are looser than current Filecoin node expectations.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Publish Filecoin-specific recommended limits instead of mutating global defaults in this module.

network_parity_audit.implication: Long-running Filecoin services need explicit resource planning in py-libp2p today.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Treat Lotus-style autoscaling/bootstrap allowlisting as a follow-up reliability/resource-management track.

network_parity_audit.implication: Bootstrap peers are not automatically protected/allowlisted in a Lotus-equivalent way.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Document the gap and prefer application-level protection/tagging for long-lived Filecoin peers.

network_parity_audit.implication: The Filecoin examples rely on runtime bootstrap dialing rather than Filecoin-shaped discovery lifecycle parity.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Keep reproducible interop probe steps explicit and defer discovery-lifecycle parity to later work.

network_parity_audit.concern: Idle connection policy and redial assumptions
network_parity_audit.implication: py-libp2p can maintain peers, but its lifecycle heuristics are still generic rather than Filecoin-specific.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Record parity notes now and treat deeper lifecycle tuning as a follow-up reliability task.

network_parity_audit.implication: Filecoin request/response workloads should not assume Lotus/Forest-sized concurrency defaults in py-libp2p.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Document the gap and keep future tuning tied to measured interop/perf work.

network_parity_audit.concern: Peer protection, bad-peer handling, and availability assumptions
network_parity_audit.implication: py-libp2p exposes building blocks, but not the same Filecoin peer-availability model.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Keep the current gap visible and do not imply Lotus/Forest-grade peer availability semantics.

network_parity_audit.concern: DHT protocol naming and routing filters
network_parity_audit.implication: The Filecoin DHT protocol helper exists, but Lotus-style routing-table/query filters are not mirrored.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Keep naming parity and document behavioural gaps.

network_parity_audit.implication: The Filecoin pubsub preset is useful today, but full scoring/gating parity remains a separate track.
network_parity_audit.suggested_change: Point users to the existing Filecoin pubsub docs/examples and keep this module focused on network parity and interop.
